articles.title: OpenAI's AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
articles.summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment, is taking medical leave for several weeks due to a neuroimmune condition. Greg Brockman will take over product responsibilities including super app efforts during her absence.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead 'special projects'
articles.summary: OpenAI announces executive changes with COO Brad Lightcap taking on a new special projects role. CMO Kate Rouch is stepping away to focus on cancer recovery with plans to return when health allows.

articles.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
articles.summary: Anthropic will no longer allow Claude subscription limits for third-party tools like OpenClaw starting April 4th. Users must switch to a separate pay-as-you-go billing option to continue using OpenClaw with Claude.

articles.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
articles.summary: Anthropic has acquired stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal. The acquisition signals Anthropic's expansion into biotechnology applications.

articles.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
articles.summary: Anthropic is currently the hottest trade in secondary markets for private shares, with OpenAI losing ground. SpaceX's potential IPO could reshape the private market landscape for AI companies.

articles.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
articles.summary: With midterm elections approaching, Anthropic has established a new political action committee. The PAC is positioned to support candidates who align with the AI company's policy agenda.

articles.title: The uphill climb of making diff lines performant
articles.summary: GitHub shares insights on optimizing diff line performance in their platform. The post emphasizes that the path to better performance is often found in simplicity rather than complex solutions.

articles.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
articles.summary: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are investing heavily in new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. The companies may face future regrets as environmental concerns and regulatory challenges emerge.

articles.title: Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing
articles.summary: Nearly 50% of data center projects are experiencing delays as China holds key components for power infrastructure. Trump's own tariff policies are contributing to the failures in his AI data center initiative.

articles.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
articles.summary: A new poll reveals public sentiment strongly favors Amazon warehouses over data centers as neighbors. The findings highlight ongoing debates about data center placement and community acceptance.

articles.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
articles.summary: As AI-generated content becomes more sophisticated, creators face skepticism when their work appears AI-made. The article suggests implementing universal labeling for human-made content, similar to Fair Trade logos.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: The viral AI agentic tool OpenClaw has a serious security vulnerability that allowed attackers to gain silent admin access without authentication. Users should assume potential compromise of their systems.

articles.title: "Cognitive surrender" leads AI users to abandon logical thinking, research finds
articles.summary: New research reveals that large majorities of AI users uncritically accept faulty AI answers. The phenomenon of "cognitive surrender" shows people abandoning their own logical thinking when using AI systems.

articles.title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 suddenly costs $650 more as RAMageddon lays waste to gaming hardware
articles.summary: Best Buy now lists the Legion Go 2 at $1,499-$1,999, representing a $650 price jump in six months. The flagship model may now cost twice as much as competing Xbox Ally X with similar AMD specifications.

articles.title: Four things we'd need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: Following SpaceX's application to launch up to one million data centers into Earth's orbit, MIT Technology Review examines the technical requirements. The analysis covers the complex challenges needed to make space-based data centers feasible.

articles.title: Scaling Reasoning Tokens via RL and Parallel Thinking: Evidence From Competitive Programming
articles.summary: Research demonstrates a log-linear relationship between validation accuracy and reasoning tokens during RL training. The study shows two methods to improve this trajectory: verification RL warmup and parallel thinking approaches.

articles.title: Procedural Knowledge at Scale Improves Reasoning
articles.summary: Introduces Reasoning Memory, a retrieval-augmented generation system that systematically reuses procedural knowledge from prior reasoning trajectories. The approach addresses how to reframe problems, choose approaches, and verify or backtrack effectively.

articles.title: No Attacker Needed: Unintentional Cross-User Contamination in Shared-State LLM Agents
articles.summary: Study reveals how LLM agents serving multiple users can unintentionally contaminate outcomes through shared knowledge layers. Information valid for one user can silently degrade another user's results when agents reapply it without proper scoping.

articles.title: Wired for Overconfidence: A Mechanistic Perspective on Inflated Verbalized Confidence in LLMs
articles.summary: Circuit-level analysis reveals why LLMs tend to be confidently wrong, verbalizing overly high confidence for incorrect answers. The research provides mechanistic understanding of inflated verbalized confidence in large language models.

articles.title: LiveMathematicianBench: A Live Benchmark for Mathematician-Level Reasoning with Proof Sketches
articles.summary: Presents a new benchmark addressing limitations of synthetic mathematical reasoning evaluations and data contamination. LiveMathematicianBench provides rigorous evaluation of LLM mathematical capabilities at mathematician level.

articles.title: From Guessing to Placeholding: A Cost-Theoretic Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Code Completion
articles.summary: Analysis of 3 million real-world interactions shows 61% of LLM code suggestions were edited or rejected despite 80% similarity. Proposes moving from hard completion paradigm to uncertainty-aware placeholding approach.

articles.title: Swift-SVD: Theoretical Optimality Meets Practical Efficiency in Low-Rank LLM Compression
articles.summary: Swift-SVD provides activation-aware SVD-based compression that is both theoretically optimal and practically efficient. The method addresses memory and bandwidth constraints in LLM deployment through hardware-friendly compression.

articles.title: The Axios supply chain attack used individually targeted social engineering
articles.summary: Analysis of a sophisticated supply chain attack that used personalized social engineering tactics. The attack demonstrates the evolution of threats beyond traditional technical vulnerabilities to human-targeted approaches.

articles.title: Vulnerability Research Is Cooked
articles.summary: Simon Willison discusses the current state and challenges facing vulnerability research. The post examines how the field may be fundamentally compromised or facing significant obstacles.

articles.title: The cognitive impact of coding agents
articles.summary: Exploration of how AI coding assistants affect developer cognition and thinking processes. The analysis examines both benefits and potential cognitive costs of relying on automated coding tools.
